integration_blocked_until: operator posts HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN in monitor/decisions/human-notes.json with approved_exp_ids containing 'EXP-212'. No strict ordering dependency on other Phase A EXPs — the new CSS uses var(--sans, fallback) / var(--mono, fallback) / var(--ink-2, fallback) / var(--rule, fallback), so it integrates cleanly with or without EXP-202 tokens. Recommended but non-blocking ordering: (a) EXP-202 before, so the demoted eyebrow picks up the --sans Inter token instead of the system-font fallback; (b) EXP-209 before, so the sc-breakdown 6-card grid is already removed and the Latest Findings demotion does not fight the bar-chart relocation; (c) EXP-211 before, so the accuracy-math sub-hero aside is settled. If EXP-212 integrates standalone, the block still visibly demotes and stays discoverable on Overview.
scope_item: Item 5 — demote 'Latest Findings' without banishing (EXP-A7 in the brief's suggested sequence). The external reviewer's F7 recommended moving the block to the References page or footer entirely; operator explicitly rejects banish, requires partial acceptance: reduce visual pitch (smaller headline, thinner chrome, no gradient) and lower placement on the Overview tab, but keep the block on Overview so the 'we track a moving target' credibility signal stays in the reader's first-visit flow.
source: HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-001 section 'SUGGESTED EXP ORDERING' step EXP-A7; external review F7 (partial acceptance per NC4).
target:
build-scripts/generate-html.js — (A) six CSS rules in the const CSS block at approximately lines 582-589 (.breaking-news, .bn-header, .bn-item, .bn-item:last-child, .bn-date, .bn-text, .bn-text strong) are rewritten to lower-pitch values; (B) the three-item
block at approximately lines 1267-1281 is moved from its current Overview position (between the Wrong Side sc-card and the sc-breakdown 6-card grid — note: the 6-card grid is itself removed by EXP-209 and replaced by the bar chart at the top of Overview) to a new position between the closing
of .verdict-legend (approximately line 1336) and the opening
(approximately line 1338); (C) the 📰 newspaper emoji inside

is removed — the new eyebrow style is text-only, uppercase with letter-spacing. test.js adds four assertions locking placement (after verdict-legend, before nav.toc), presence (three bn-item entries survive), chrome weight (border:1px, no gradient, no accent-color header), and emoji removal.
kernel_of_truth: The external reviewer's F7 correctly identifies that the Latest Findings block sits at comparable visual weight to the primary analysis surface — it has an accent-colored 2px border, a rounded gradient background, and a 1.15rem accent-colored headline with a newspaper emoji. Collectively that chrome says 'BREAKING NEWS' before the reader has parsed a word of the content. On a document whose credibility thesis is restraint ('a debunking document's strongest credibility signal is restraint'), a news-ticker aesthetic works against us. The reviewer is also right that dated entries at the top of the document frame the review as reactive, chasing the subject. Where the reviewer gets it wrong is the prescribed remedy (banish to References page or footer): the Latest Findings block is content, not chrome. It is evidence that the document tracks a moving target, which is itself a credibility signal — we are demonstrably keeping up with the dome site's changes rather than freezing a snapshot and defending it. NC4 (operator) makes this explicit. The right answer is therefore demote (reduce visual pitch + move lower on Overview), not banish. This EXP writes exactly that.

content_diff.description:
Two coordinated changes in generate-html.js plus four test.js assertions. (1) CSS rewrite: the .breaking-news container loses its 2px accent border, 10px radius, and gradient background; gains a 1px rule border, 6px radius, and flat card-bg background. .bn-header loses its 1.15rem accent-colored styling; gains a .78rem uppercase letter-spaced eyebrow with var(--ink-2) / var(--sans) (both use var() with system-font fallback so the rule works pre-EXP-202). .bn-date gains var(--mono, fallback) for date alignment. .bn-text drops from .93rem to .85rem. Section margin grows top (2rem), shrinks bottom (1rem) to push the block visually below primary content. (2) Block relocation: the
...
block moves from immediately after the .sc-hero + Wrong Side sc-card pair to between the .verdict-legend closing and the
opening. The 📰 emoji (📰) inside

is removed; the new eyebrow text is 'LATEST FINDINGS' (the uppercase is rendered by CSS text-transform, the string in HTML stays mixed-case 'Latest Findings'). Block contents (the three .bn-item entries) are otherwise preserved byte-for-byte.

change_1_css_rewrite.where: build-scripts/generate-html.js, inside the const CSS = ` ... `; block at approximately lines 582-589.
change_1_css_rewrite.rationale: Reduces visual pitch on every selector that carries chrome weight: border, background, margin, font-size, color. Preserves structural selectors (.breaking-news, .bn-header, .bn-item, .bn-date, .bn-text) so the HTML contract does not change — only the pixels change. Uses var() with fallbacks so the rule is integration-order independent relative to EXP-202.
deltas: .breaking-news margin: 1.5rem 0 → 2rem 0 1rem  (top margin grows to push block below primary content; bottom shrinks so it tucks neatly against the following nav.toc)

.breaking-news background: linear-gradient(135deg,var(--card-bg),rgba(42,100,150,0.04)) → var(--card-bg)  (gradient is the single biggest 'news-site' signal; flat card-bg reads as subordinate container)
.bn-header color: var(--accent) → var(--ink-2,#888)  (accent color was the second strongest 'hero' signal; ink-2 / muted grey is eyebrow weight)
.bn-header text-transform: none → uppercase  (eyebrow typographic convention for supplementary/secondary info)
.bn-header letter-spacing: normal → .08em  (paired with uppercase is a standard 'section metadata' type role)
.bn-header font-family: inherit → var(--sans,...)  (chrome role per Item 6 typography system)
.bn-date font-family: inherit → var(--mono,...)  (dates are numeric/tabular data — mono role per Item 6)
.bn-text color: inherit → var(--text)  (explicit so strong inside inherits correctly even without the strong selector)
.bn-text strong font-weight: implicit 700 → 600  (de-emphasis within the de-emphasized block)
@media print background: inherited → transparent  (flat card-bg would render as a grey rectangle in print; transparent is cleaner)

change_2_block_relocation.where:
build-scripts/generate-html.js Overview tab template; the
...
block at approximately lines 1267-1281 moves.
change_2_block_relocation.rationale: Item 5 requires that the block be 'visibly subordinate to the stance statement, the hero thesis, and the verdict distribution chart, but is still discoverable on the overview without a tab switch.' After EXP-208 (stance) and EXP-209 (bar chart) integrate, the top of the Overview is: masthead + stance → bar chart → hero scorecard + Wrong Side card → accuracy-math aside (EXP-211) → Downloads → Verdict Categories. Placing the demoted Latest Findings block after the Verdict Categories legend (which is reference material, not primary argument) and before the Table of Contents nav puts it at the bottom of the Overview tab's content surface without leaving the tab. A reader who scrolls the Overview top-to-bottom meets primary argument first, then reference material, then 'recent updates' just before the TOC lets them choose what to read next — which is the natural place for 'what's new' in a document whose structure the reader is about to navigate.

change_2_block_relocation.byte_preservation: The three .bn-item entries (2026-04-17 timestamp narrative, 2026-04-12 refraction fix coordinate collapse, 2026-04-10 predictions catalog) are preserved byte-for-byte including their anchor targets, onclick handlers, and the template expression ${context.predCounts.total} etc. Only (a) the block's position in the source and (b) the <h2 class="bn-header"> content changes.
change_2_block_relocation.h2_header_change:
📰 Latest Findings

→

Latest Findings

(the 📰 newspaper emoji is removed; visual de-emphasis is now carried by CSS eyebrow-style, not by decorative chrome).

change_3_test_js_assertions.where: test.js, appended to the existing assertion block.
change_3_test_js_assertions.rationale: Four lockdown tests for the post-Phase-A structure. Each is narrowly worded to catch regression (someone reverting to the old styling or moving the block back to the top) without over-specifying (we don't assert exact hex values in case EXP-202 tokens shift the semantic palette).

assertions.name: latest_findings_below_verdict_legend
assertions.purpose: Locks placement; catches anyone reverting to pre-A7 position (between Wrong Side card and sc-breakdown).

assertions.name: latest_findings_has_three_items_and_preserved_links
assertions.purpose: Locks content preservation; catches accidental deletion of a bn-item or breakage of its navigation link.

assertions.name: latest_findings_chrome_weight_reduced
assertions.purpose: Locks chrome reduction. Any revert to the pre-A7 hero styling (2px accent border, gradient background, 1.15rem accent headline) fails the test.

assertions.name: latest_findings_emoji_removed
assertions.purpose: Locks emoji removal. Decorative chrome removed; uppercase eyebrow carries the visual role.

acceptance_criteria: An Overview reader scrolls top-to-bottom: masthead + stance statement → verdict distribution bar chart → hero scorecard + Wrong Side card → accuracy-math aside → Downloads → Verdict Categories → demoted Latest Findings → Table of Contents. The Latest Findings block is clearly subordinate in pitch (smaller headline, thinner border, no gradient, no emoji) but still present on Overview without a tab switch.
CSS audit: .breaking-news uses 1px border (not 2px), has no linear-gradient background, and its header is not accent-colored.
.bn-header is styled as an uppercase eyebrow (text-transform:uppercase, letter-spacing, ink-2 or muted grey color).
No 📰 newspaper emoji (📰) in the .bn-header text — the header reads 'Latest Findings' as plain text.
All three bn-item entries are preserved byte-for-byte (dates 2026-04-17, 2026-04-12, 2026-04-10 and their 'Full update →' / 'Full analysis →' / 'See the full catalog →' anchor links).
Build-reproducibility check (NC6) passes: fresh clone + node build.js html produces identical docs/index.html up to embedded date line.
test.js passes with the four new assertions (placement, three-item preservation, chrome weight reduction, emoji removal).
NC4 (Latest Findings stays on Overview) preserved: block is still inside the Overview tab content div, not moved to References page or footer.
Tap-target / WCAG AA audit (EXP-A8 scope) can still detect the block — the demoted styling does not remove the links.

anticipated_objections.objection: Demoting Latest Findings below the Verdict Categories legend pushes it far enough down that casual readers who don't scroll to the bottom of Overview will never see it. That destroys the 'we track a moving target' credibility signal that NC4 explicitly protects.
anticipated_objections.response: Three points. (1) Overview scroll length post-A2/A3/A4/A5/A6 integration is shorter than the current Overview, not longer — EXP-209 deletes the six-card sc-breakdown (~12 lines of visual real estate) and EXP-211 collapses the accuracy-math bordered box into a sub-hero aside. The net effect is that the distance from 'hero scorecard' to 'Latest Findings' in its new position is comparable to the distance from 'hero scorecard' to the CURRENT Latest Findings position, not dramatically farther. (2) The block remains on the default-open tab with no interaction required. 'Below the fold' is not the same as 'hidden' — a reader who scrolls to find the TOC naturally passes over it. (3) The acceptance criterion in the brief is 'visibly subordinate to the stance statement, the hero thesis, and the verdict distribution chart' — that is a directive to demote. If the block needs to stay above-the-fold to be credible, we would have failed the brief. The operator-authored NC4 is about not relocating to References page or footer, not about keeping it above-the-fold.

anticipated_objections.objection: Using uppercase + letter-spacing for the .bn-header is a typographic convention most associated with advertising and marketing sites. A restraint-focused debunking document should avoid it precisely because it reads as 'chrome trying to look editorial.'
anticipated_objections.response: The uppercase-eyebrow convention is also the dominant pattern in editorial journalism (New York Times section labels, The Atlantic department headers, longform academic journals' sidebar labels) — it is genre-appropriate for 'this is supplementary metadata, not the main argument.' The alternative (plain lowercase grey text) risks the opposite failure: looking like an unstyled paragraph rather than a deliberate demotion. The 1.15rem accent-colored bn-header currently reads as a section HERO; the .78rem muted uppercase eyebrow reads as a section LABEL. Both states are intentional visual choices; neither is the 'no design at all' option. If the human review group prefers plain lowercase, the text-transform:uppercase and letter-spacing:.08em declarations can be removed without affecting placement or chrome reduction — this is a cadence knob.

anticipated_objections.objection: Removing the 📰 emoji loses the 'scan for updates' affordance. A reader scrolling fast can spot the newspaper icon and know 'there's news here'; a text-only eyebrow is more likely to be skimmed past.
anticipated_objections.response: Acknowledged: the emoji did earn its weight for scanability. The tradeoff is deliberate. The brief's Item 7 (palette red restriction) plus its NC1 (dark theme stays as credibility differentiator) plus the overall thesis ('restraint is the credibility signal') converge on removing decorative emoji from chrome surfaces. The NYT-style eyebrow is the scan-replacement: uppercase letter-spaced text is itself a scan-for-metadata affordance once the reader learns the convention. If the human group wants to preserve scanability via a non-emoji glyph (e.g., a small icon in a sprite, or a border-left accent rule specifically on the Latest Findings block), that is a reasonable Phase B addition — but it should be a deliberate design token, not a decorative emoji.

anticipated_objections.objection: The new margin (2rem 0 1rem) tucks the block against the nav.toc with only 1rem of bottom space. That's too tight — the TOC needs its own 'air' to read as a separate navigational element.
anticipated_objections.response: The nav.toc has its own margin-top from the existing .toc rule (background, border, padding 1.2rem 1.5rem, margin 1.5rem 0). Combined with .breaking-news margin-bottom of 1rem, the separation between the last bn-item border-bottom and the TOC outer border is 1rem (from .breaking-news) + 1.5rem (from .toc margin-top) = 2.5rem of visual air. That is deliberate and comparable to the current spacing between the sc-breakdown grid and the accuracy-math box (1.5rem + 1.5rem = 3rem). If the tuning turns out to need more air after visual inspection, the fix is a one-value tweak to .breaking-news margin-bottom — flagged in notes_for_human_review_group.

anticipated_objections.objection: The var() fallback chain (var(--rule,var(--border)) etc.) is defensive programming against integration ordering. It clutters the CSS and makes the rule harder to reason about after EXP-202 integrates, when the fallbacks are unreachable.
anticipated_objections.response: Cleanup pass after all Phase A EXPs integrate is the right time to strip fallbacks. That is one line in a future integrity-run or a tinker-pass EXP. The fallbacks exist because the HARD HOLD model permits operator to approve EXPs in arbitrary order (see the integration_blocked_until language — operator decides order). A rule that depends on token X but renders garbage if token X is missing creates a live production hazard for exactly one integration round. The fallback cost is six extra commas and six extra default values; the benefit is order-independence for a foundational design refresh. Cheap insurance.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_: CC1 (Kill Shots is not chrome): Not applicable to EXP-212. The Kill Shots tab, label, and styling are untouched. This EXP is scoped to the Latest Findings block inside the Overview tab only.

CC2 (4+4 failure accounting reachable-in-one-scroll from Overview): Preserved. The accuracy-math aside (EXP-211) remains on Overview between the hero scorecard and the Downloads block. The 4+4 failure sc-card border-lefts are neutralized by EXP-210 (palette red-restriction). Relocating Latest Findings below the Verdict Categories legend does NOT push the accuracy-math calculation below the fold — the accuracy-math aside is still above the Verdict Categories legend, which is above the relocated Latest Findings block.

CC3 (Latest Findings is content, not news-ticker chrome — demotion must preserve discoverability): The EXP's whole design turns on this distinction. Demotion here means reducing visual pitch (CSS chrome) while preserving content identity (three bn-item entries, their timestamps, their links) and tab placement (still on Overview). A reader who scrolls the Overview top-to-bottom encounters the block naturally. Banish to References page or footer is explicitly rejected by NC4; this EXP does not do that.

CC4 (dark theme differentiator): Preserved. All new CSS values use tokens (var(--rule,var(--border)), var(--ink-2,#888), var(--text), var(--card-bg)) that already have dark-theme overrides in the existing :root @media(prefers-color-scheme:dark) block. The fallbacks (#888, 'Segoe UI', etc.) also work on dark. No hardcoded light-theme hex values are introduced.
CC5 (hero thesis sentence is a CANDIDATE): Not applicable to EXP-212. The hero thesis is deferred per Item 1. This EXP does not touch the masthead or introduce any hero thesis text.

CC6 (computed counts rule — derive from wins.json): Preserved. The existing three bn-item entries use the template expressions ${context.predCounts.total}, ${context.predCounts.deadOnArrival}, ${context.predCounts.stdRelabel}, ${context.predCounts.ourFalsified}, ${context.predCounts.ourRecycled}, ${context.predCounts.ourUnfalsifiable} — these flow through the existing build pipeline. EXP-212 does not change the content of any bn-item, only their block's position and chrome.
CC7 (stance statement operator-authored, four load-bearing moves non-negotiable): Not applicable to EXP-212. The masthead is untouched.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.NC1_dark_theme: All new CSS uses tokens that already have dark-theme overrides; no light-theme hex values introduced.
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.NC2_tabs_stay: The block moves WITHIN the Overview tab — it does not escape to another tab or to a long-form scroll layout. Tab IA untouched.
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.NC3_kill_shots_term: Untouched — this EXP does not visit Kill Shots content.
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.NC4_latest_findings_stays_on_overview: EXPLICITLY HONORED. The block is relocated WITHIN Overview, not to References or footer. The F7 banish recommendation is rejected; demote is the substitute.
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.NC5_computed_counts: All three bn-item entries keep their existing ${context.predCounts.*} template expressions. No hardcoded counts introduced.
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.NC6_build_reproducibility: No timestamp generation, no randomness, no external fetch. The CSS rewrite and the block relocation are static source edits. Fresh clone + node build.js html produces deterministic output.
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.NC7_tests_green: Four new test.js assertions added; none of the existing 3400+ assertions are modified or invalidated. Net: test.js count grows by 4.

preserved_load_bearing_content: All three bn-item entries (timestamps and full prose) preserved byte-for-byte.
All three intra-document links preserved: #ts-april-2026-update (with onclick handler to open the timestamp tab), #section-1-8, #pred-mined.
The header text 'Latest Findings' is preserved (only the 📰 emoji prefix is removed).
The template expressions ${context.predCounts.total}, .deadOnArrival, .stdRelabel, .ourFalsified, .ourRecycled, .ourUnfalsifiable remain intact in bn-item #3.
The @media print override for the block is preserved (and improved: now explicitly sets background:transparent for cleaner print output).
out_of_scope_for_this_exp: The sc-breakdown 6-card grid removal and 'Where the ${counts.total} claims actually land:' caption removal — that is EXP-209 (EXP-A4).
The accuracy-math bordered callout demotion to sub-hero aside — that is EXP-211 (EXP-A6).
The masthead stance statement — that is EXP-208 (EXP-A3).
The hero thesis sentence — deferred per Item 1; candidate text lives in EXP-208 notes_for_human_review_group.

Red-chrome neutralization on stat cards, kill-shot badges, FAIL-NNN entry cards — that is EXP-210 (EXP-A5). This EXP removes the .breaking-news gradient (which uses rgba(42,100,150,0.04) — a blue-accent tint, not red) and the .bn-header accent-color — but those are separate chrome surfaces.
A11y audit, glyph+label pairing, 44px tap targets, mobile stacked cards — that is EXP-A8 (next in suggested sequence).

Typography token application across all prose/chrome — that is EXP-207 (EXP-A2). This EXP uses var(--sans, fallback) and var(--mono, fallback) only on two selectors (.bn-header, .bn-date) to assign Item 6 roles locally; global typography application is A2's job.
A cleanup pass to strip var() fallbacks after all Phase A EXPs integrate — a future separate EXP.
Any change to the Latest Findings block contents (adding or removing bn-item entries, editing their text). New entries land via normal content workflow, not as part of this design-refresh EXP.
Any addition of a 'What would change our mind' module on Overview — F9/R3 is SUBSUMED by EXP-190 scoreboard per the brief.
notes_for_human_review_group: Placement choice (between verdict-legend and nav.toc): this is a judgment call. The alternative placements considered were (a) between the accuracy-math aside and Downloads, and (b) between Downloads and the Verdict Categories legend. (a) was rejected because it reduces the visual gap between accuracy-math and the 'news' block in a way that made the accuracy-math feel like it was attached to the news block. (b) was rejected because the Downloads block is itself low-prominence and putting Latest Findings between them produces a 'low-prominence sandwich' that reads as footer material. Placing Latest Findings AFTER the Verdict Categories legend means the reader ends their content read with 'here's what's new' and then moves into the navigation TOC — a narrative flow of argument → reference → updates → navigation. If the human group prefers (a) or (b), the relocation is a one-line source move.

Uppercase + letter-spacing eyebrow (text-transform:uppercase; letter-spacing:.08em): cadence knob. The human group may prefer plain lowercase muted grey — both achieve visual demotion, the uppercase-eyebrow choice is a typographic convention signal. Removing the two declarations downgrades the header from 'deliberately demoted with a visual convention' to 'just smaller and greyer.'

Emoji removal: judgment call. The brief's Item 7 is about red, not emoji, so strictly speaking 📰 is safe (it's a chrome decoration, but not a red one). The removal is a design-consistency choice: once we commit to eyebrow-style header text, an emoji prefix reads as inconsistent chrome. If the human group wants to keep the 📰, put it back in

📰 Latest Findings

and adjust the emoji-removal test assertion.

Monospace font on dates (var(--mono, fallback) for .bn-date): this commits to Item 6's 'numeric data uses mono' rule locally in EXP-212 rather than waiting for EXP-207 to apply typography globally. If EXP-207 integrates first the fallback is moot; if EXP-212 integrates first, dates render in the specified fallback stack (ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Consolas, Liberation Mono, monospace). Either way the dates read as tabular data. Human group may prefer to defer the mono assignment to EXP-207 — in which case .bn-date loses its font-family line and picks up the global mono assignment when EXP-207 lands.

Block border color (var(--rule,var(--border))): the fallback uses the existing --border token, which is #ccc light / #444 dark. The --rule token (EXP-202) is intended to be slightly softer than --border. If EXP-202 integrates first, the demotion gets slightly softer; if EXP-212 first, the fallback is acceptable. Both values pass WCAG non-text contrast for border/divider lines.
Build-reproducibility: the proposed CSS rewrite is static string replacement. There is no source of nondeterminism. If integrity detects a reproducibility failure, the cause will not be this EXP.

Test.js assertions: four assertions as specified above. None are load-bearing on values that EXP-202 will later rewrite — the chrome_weight_reduced assertion checks 'no linear-gradient' and 'no accent color' which will remain stable post-EXP-202.

Phase A dependency ladder: with EXP-212 drafted, Phase A Items 1–5 and 7 all have DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW expansions (EXP-208 Item 1, EXP-202 Item 6/7 tokens foundation, EXP-207 Item 6 typography, EXP-209 Item 2 bar chart, EXP-210 Item 7 palette, EXP-211 Item 4 accuracy-math, EXP-212 Item 5 Latest Findings). Only Item 3 (a11y audit + fixes, EXP-A8) remains to be drafted. After EXP-A8 the full Phase A draft set is ready for batched or per-EXP operator approval.

sibling_exps: EXP-202 (EXP-A1 tokens) — tokens for var(--rule), var(--ink-2), var(--sans), var(--mono) used here with fallbacks
EXP-207 (EXP-A2 typography) — global typography application; overlaps locally on .bn-header and .bn-date font-family assignments
EXP-208 (EXP-A3 stance statement) — masthead; referenced in placement reasoning
EXP-209 (EXP-A4 bar chart) — replaces sc-breakdown; EXP-212 placement assumes the sc-breakdown is gone
EXP-210 (EXP-A5 palette) — removes red chrome elsewhere; EXP-212 removes blue gradient chrome
EXP-211 (EXP-A6 accuracy-math aside) — precedes EXP-212 block in Overview flow
EXP-A8 a11y fixes (not yet drafted) — will pair glyph+label with any color-coded affordances introduced here
